item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The idea of a predatory car is legitimately hilarious, and this perfectly toes the line between horror and comedy in a way that was really enjoyable to read. Solid upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow, I think this might be one of my favorite SCPs I've read after all these years. The pacing is great and the gradual reveal of just how organic this "car" is was just great. Also, the investigation reports were both serious and interesting, but had moments that actually made me laugh out loud. But the part where
+ Spoilers!
- Spoilers~

the second instance of the SCP is revealed was really good!

Very awesome! +1

item.title: Inconsistency
item.content:encoded:
Here we have "Lambda-12 quickly exits the cave. SCP-3470 does not appear as they exit the area; however, L5 and L6 could not be found and did not respond to any radio transmissions following the egress of the rest of Lambda-12."

But in the main entry on MTFs, we have "Task Force Mission: Specializes in tracking, containing, and exterminating anomalous vermin. Often used as a first-response team when tracking anomalous organisms. Have never lost an agent on any of their missions."

So, they've never lost an agent, but they lost two here. That doesn't fit. One is wrong.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this one. The idea is unique and the dialogue flows well. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love the dialogue here. That's what made it for me, personally.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's a "flying Ford Anglia" in Book 2, Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets. It is somewhat intelligent (exactly how intelligent, we don't know) and chases some giant spiders at one point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love how batshit crazy this is, and the clinical tone fits very well, given Foundation standards. For a moment i got scared they were going to say "grab half, leave the rest"

"why the fu- oh burn them… ye"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay, I hate to necro, but because nobody else has brought it up, I feel like I have to - what in the name of [REDACTED] does this have to do with Harry Potter? I don't quite recall the part where Harry was chased by a killer truck.
Excellent! I've always had a soft spot for this killer car, and I hope to bring back D-01 and D-13 again soon!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this article, but I knew I felt something was off. When you put it that way, damn. I think it makes way more sense to go with the equipment you mentioned.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This skip! It must be the work of an enemy Stand!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really? You send these guys up against a friggin' car carrying a tanq gun and a net launcher? Google Man VS Car and come back. Admittedly, the flamethrower came in handy because apparently Car Eggs aren't made of metal for some reason but come on. What was the net launcher supposed to do against even a juvenile CAR!? Where's the EMP? Where's the easily-deployed strips of tire spikes? Where's the sniper on overwatch with armor-peircing explosive rounds? Where's the industrial grade electromagnet on a crane?

Shooting nets at a car. Bastards are lucky the arm was even able to feel pain. Shooting nets at a car!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Huh, for once the D-Class aren't reduced to bloody piles of gore. I like that. Honestly, I feel D-Class are killed off too frequently, so I like that they survived unharmed (ignoring any mental trauma).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is definitely a creepy one. Just imagine that a nice looking car drives up to you, but then a hand with a pair of goddamn eyeballs pops out the window and stares at you before eating you alive. Definitely not an experience you would want to have during your first drive. +1
Also is it just me, or is this thing literally Wheel of Fortune from Jojo's Bizarre Adventure?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can think of worse ways to die than being chased down and eaten by a 50 year old British coupe, but not many weirder ways. I'm so glad we have this in the main list now. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"I refuse to be killed by a goddamn Ford".

That's an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Christine was a car possessed by a ghost. This one is literally a living organism that hunts and reproduces, incidentally using the form of a car to draw in prey.

If anything, it resembles the creature from King's Mile 81.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …isn't it basically Christine, except Ford Anglia instead of Plymouth?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
D-01 and D-13 need tale series!

Seriously the dialog really sold this one for me, a lot of fun and interesting imagery.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Could probably use a photo of a Ford Anglia to go with this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: L5 and L6.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really want more logs added to this! +1 beep beep!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I dig it. We have no shortage of mimicry predators on this site, but this article still entertained, especially with the logs at the end. I personally feel that the brevity of the article works in its favor, as its not allowed to overstay its welcome and spin its wheels.

Either way, +1. Well done ARD.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this one.

The skip is a classic Series 1 feeling monster, and the logs have a lot of character.

And you fight the cliche by having nobody die. Which actually makes sense, it's not that dangerous if you know it's coming and what it does.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"I refuse to be killed by a goddamn Ford."

Your +1 was guaranteed at that point. I agree with Leviritas that the contrasting dialogue worked well. I also like that nobody got killed by a goddamn Ford.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is great. The logs add to it as well, the dialogue is natural, especially since the phrasing of the D-class and the MTF are contrasted here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, they do have to figure out how to get him into the driver's seat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I absolutely love the application of clinical tone here. Something so crazy is described perfectly, and this could honestly be one of the examples I show newbies in the future when they've got tone issues.

That being said, the article itself is a bit shallow. I'm with Roget on this, that it could use some development, in improving the impact, but it's still an easy upvote for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The main anomaly here doesn't feel fully developed in the article, the logs built on top of it are okay but I never really felt any tension or gravity to them. The dialogue and general tone all read like stuff I've seen here before. Maybe a little more time devoted to the spooky car and laying out a more defined foundation for the logs could improve it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Psh, why send an MTF when Dr. Gerald can easily neutralize this on his own?

In all seriousness, wonderfully weird and excellent logs. +1

item.title: DRIVE SAFELY KIDS
item.content:encoded:
I've been doing a lot of short articles lately. This is one of those - conceived and written up within six hours. While driving home from the gym I saw my brother and waved to him. Then I thought about how weird that looked and was like - damn it would be even weirder if there were eyes in the palm of my hand. Plus I've wanted to write a tale now where the MTFs and the D-class survive by being competent.

Many thanks to Taffeta, Shaggydredlocks, Modern_Erasmus, and not_a_seagull for critique.

I hope you enjoy! See more of my work on my author page!
